name: Ornette Coleman
description: American jazz saxophonist, violinist, trumpeter, and composer (1930–2015)
abstract: Randolph Denard Ornette Coleman (March 9, 1930 – June 11, 2015) was an American jazz saxophonist, trumpeter, violinist, and composer. He is best known as a principal founder of the free jazz genre, a term derived from his 1960 album Free Jazz: A Collective Improvisation.

The Sacred Mind of Johnny Dolphin

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: That‟s where I met Ornette Coleman, because Mel told me, “There‟s a kid in town. He‟s been going around to all the sessions, and they won‟t let him play, because they say he doesn‟t know how to play.” He showed up a few times, and we said “Yeah, we‟ll let him play.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: I bought everything that had a saxophone on it. And I didn’t know, you know I had everything from of course Charlie Parker, Phil Woods, Ornette Coleman... I heard Ornette Coleman playing the violin I thought my God what is he doing, I had no idea you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Karolina Strassmayer interviewed by Monk Rowe, May 25, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Drummond
about_quotes.evidence: Ornette Coleman was the same way. It took me a little longer for Ornette, but the first record I bought from Ornette... I didn't understand what that was all about. And then one day I put one of Ornette’s... on and said oh man, whoa, where have I been?
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Drummond interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Ingrid Sertso
about_quotes.evidence: Ornette invented the harmolodic approach to music and I think he helped us all to be confident in our need to really express ourselves freely... Ornette is like a guru to us. He’s like a guru. He kept us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ingrid Sertso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, August 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: George Russell
about_quotes.evidence: when I heard Ornette, it opened the door to supra-vertical, all and everything, because he was something.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Russell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: Coltrane and Ornette Coleman and Cecil Taylor and Stockhausen and Bartok were my mentors.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
